page: Microsoft Azure D64s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 vCPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading) operating at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA instructions. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower scores in stress-ng single-core, geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression. However, the server demonstrates average performance in multi-core workloads, including stress-ng multi-core, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and software compilation. Notably, memory bandwidth for large blocks is strong, placing in the top tier, which correlates with strong performance in Passmark CPU extended instructions.

Given its resource density, the D64s_v3 offers a balanced hardware profile for workloads requiring substantial memory and parallel processing without needing specialized GPU acceleration. The qualitative cost efficiency is typical of standard general-purpose instances, where the cost of dedicated resources is offset by high memory bandwidth and multi-core capacity. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM prompt processing, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: The Microsoft Azure D64s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 server features 32 physical cores, a 2.6 GHz clock speed, and 524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage support. Performance benchmarks show a contrast between weak single-core execution and average multi-core capabilities, though it achieves strong results in large-block memory bandwidth and extended CPU instructions. Lacking GPU acceleration, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that scale horizontally across its 64 vCPUs. It is best suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale language model prompt processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, offering strong large-block memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64s_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 524 GB local storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual machine featuring high memory bandwidth and dedicated compute resources for parallelized enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
256.0 GB of RAM providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
524 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong large-block memory read and write bandwidth performance
Best suited for database operations, multi-threaded compilation, and web serving
